mission.title: Install and Configure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS)
mission.description: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) is a managed Kubernetes offering that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on IBM Cloud. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated IBM Cloud services. Key trade-offs include reliance on IBM's infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the IBM Cloud CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the IBM Cloud CLI, which is required to interact with IBM Cloud services.

```bash

curl -sL https://ibm.biz/idt-installer | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the IBM Cloud CLI.

steps.title: Login to IBM Cloud
steps.description:
Authenticate to your IBM Cloud account to manage resources.

```bash

ibmcloud login

```

> Follow the prompts to enter your IBM Cloud credentials.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-create --name my-cluster --zone us-south-1 --kube-version 1.30 --num-workers 2

```

> Adjust the zone and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local environment to use the new cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IBM-Cloud/kube-samples/master/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IBM-Cloud/kube-samples/master/monitoring.yaml

```

> This applies an example autoscaler configuration.
> This applies an example monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-delete --cluster my-cluster --force

```

> Use --force to skip confirmation.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

ibmcloud resource service-instance-delete my-load-balancer

```

> Replace with your actual load balancer name.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, upgrade to the new version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the specified Kubernetes version.
> Verify that nodes are in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of worker nodes using 'ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --num-workers <new-count>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the user does not have permission to access the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are logged in with the correct IBM Cloud account using 'ibmcloud login'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a pod is failing to start due to application errors.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes features such as PodSecurity admission and improved scheduling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs related to networking.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review and update any deprecated API usages in your configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the IBM Cloud CLI and kubectl installed before proceeding.
